sutta: [Music] 1 is the verbal sariputta dwelt near rajya gaha at the squirrel's feeding ground in the bamboo grove and there he addressed the monk saying this school nibana reverences his happiness this cool references his happiness now when he had spoken thus the vulnerable udain said to him but what hearing reverence is the happiness which hearing is not sensed stop here for a moment nibana is the state now where the senses have stopped them so this mangudais wants the variable sarikuta to and the variables should sarkuta continue it is very very just that happiness which hearing is not sensed there are references these 5 strands of sense desire what 5 there are forms cognized by the eye longed for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire there are sounds cognized by the ear smells by the nose tastes by the tongue contacts or tangibles recognized by the touch longed for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire these ribbons are the 5 strands of sense desire and the happiness the well-being arising there from is called sensual happiness consider reverence the monk who allude from sense desire aloo from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first jhana if while he abides in that abiding perceptions and thoughts accompanied by sense desire beset him it is indeed for him a disease just as some ill amounting to a disease might arise in a happy person even so those perceptions and thoughts accompanied by sense desire beset him and indeed it is for him a disease now disease is called ill by the exalted 1 verily reverend sir it is just in this way that the cool nirvana ought to be understood as happiness stop here for a moment so here uh the variable salary putter is explaining that when among enters the first ghana he abides in a very peaceful and a very blissful abiding and if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by sense desire should be set him it sort of gets him out of the first jana and it is like a happy person becoming sick so that is why um some disease so so uh saying that the cool banner ought to be understood as happiness in this way in other words he is likening the state of the first jhana itself to bana is very happy blissful abiding and he continues again consider the monk who enters and abides in the second jhana his perceptions and thoughts accompanied by application that means applied beside him disease is just this way that the could talk to in the third journal exceptions and the thoughts accompanied by delight pt beside him or in the fourth jhana if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by pain beset him or in this or in the sphere of infinite space if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by form beset him or in the sphere of infinite consciousness if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by the sphere of infinite space beset him or in the sphere of nothingness if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by the sphere of infinite consciousness beset him or in the sphere of neither perception or non-perception if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by this fear of nothingness beset him it is indeed for him a disease filled ill amounting to a disease might arise in a happy person even so those things beset him and indeed they are for him diseases now disease is called ill by the exalted 1 verily reverence sir it is just in this way that the cool ought to be understood as happiness and then reverence consider the monk who passing wholly beyond the sphere of neither perception or non-perception enters and abides in the ending of perception and feeling when by wisdom he sees that the asavas are completely destroyed clearly reference it is just in this way that the cool ought to be understood as happiness that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
so similarly variable sarikuta is saying if a person abides in the jhana and some perceptions and thoughts accompanied by a lower state arises so that it moves him out him out of that ghana state it is just like a happy man becoming sigma he's he's moved out of that blissful state now so those things that move him out of the blissful state are a disease so that is why the verbal sarikuta is giving the similaria that when the manga bites in the jhanas it is it is such a blissful happy statement similar to nirvana in a way yeah so you can see the arahants and the buddha themselves they sometimes liken the jhana states to nibana
